title: Simple Modern Outdoor Sectional
summary: A modular 2x4 patio sofa — build one armless seat at a time, add corner pieces, and grow it to fit your space. All exterior screws, no fancy joinery.
description:
This is a whole outdoor sectional broken into one repeatable module: an armless seat you build as many times as you need, plus a corner piece to turn the run. That modularity is the point — start with two seats, add a corner and a third later, and it all bolts together into one couch.

The construction is deliberately blunt: 2x4s, two 2-1/2" exterior self-tapping screws per joint, and exterior glue. Self-tapping screws let you drive straight into 2x4s without pre-drilling and splitting, and 'exterior' matters at every level — the screws, the glue, and ideally a rot-resistant or well-sealed wood, because this lives in the weather.

The seat slats space evenly across the frame so rain drains and the cushions breathe. When you build the corner, pay attention to which side the seat apron board lands on, because the corner can face either direction depending on how your sectional wraps — decide the layout first, then build the corner to match. Size cushions after the frames are done; a 2x4 seat is close to standard outdoor cushion depth.
timeLabel: 4–7 hrs for one seat plus a corner

tools.note: Self-tapping screws mean no pre-drilling for most joints.
Ease every edge — bare arms and legs rest here.

materials.name: 2x4 board, 8 ft (corner)
materials.note: Plus a 4 ft length for the corner piece.

materials.name: Outdoor seat and back cushions
materials.note: Sized after the frames are built.

cutList.note: Spaced evenly so rain drains and cushions breathe.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two seat frame, long at 24", two seat frame, short at 22", two front legs at 13-1/2", two back legs at 27-1/2", one back top rail at 25", seven seat slats at 22", two corner legs at 27-1/2", one corner top at 28-1/2" and two corner seat and apron at 23-1/2" from the 2x4. Cut two seat trim at 25" from the 2x2. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to six 8 ft 2x4. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build the seat frame
steps.body: Assemble the seat frame from the long and short 2x4s, two 2-1/2" self-tapping screws per joint and exterior glue. Check it square — every later part references this frame.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Fasten the front and back legs to the frame from the inside, two screws per joint plus glue. The taller back legs form the backrest posts.

steps.title: Add the back top rail
steps.body: Screw the top rail across the back legs with 2-1/2" screws — this sets the backrest and stiffens the whole piece.

steps.title: Lay the seat slats
steps.body: Place the two outer slats and the center slat first and screw them down, two screws per joint, then space the remaining slats evenly between them and fasten. Even gaps drain rain and let the cushions breathe.

steps.title: Add the seat trim
steps.body: Fasten the 2x2 trim to the seat slats and into the legs where you can reach, to finish off the end of the seat and cover the slat ends.

steps.title: Build the corner and join modules
steps.body: Build the corner piece, watching which side the seat apron lands on — the corner works on either hand depending on how your sectional wraps, so set your layout first. Attach the corner to a seat module's legs with 2-1/2" screws. Sand all edges, seal or let it silver, and size cushions to the finished frames.

images.alt: Modular 2x4 outdoor sectional seat with cushions
